description: Pythagoras is reported by ancient sources to have travelled to Babylon. The Babylonian mathematical tradition — particularly its interest in numerical ratios, musical intervals, and the properties of right triangles (the 'Pythagorean' theorem was known to Babylonian mathematicians over a thousand years before Pythagoras) — almost certainly informed Pythagorean number mysticism. The leap from 'numbers are useful tools' (Babylonian) to 'number is the fundamental nature of reality' (Pythagorean) is a philosophical transformation of a mathematical inheritance.
source: Iamblichus, Life of Pythagoras; Eleanor Robson, Mathematics in Ancient Iraq (2008)
